# R2 — Multi-Mode Reading Views

> **Milestone**: M1.4 | **Epic**: E1.4.2 | **Features**: F1.4.2.1 + F1.4.2.2
> **Tasks**: T1.4.2.1.1.1 + T1.4.2.2.1.1
> **Depends on**: nothing — parallel with R1, R3
> **Skills to load**: `flutter-managing-state`, `riverpod-providers`, `riverpod-codegen-and-hooks`, `flutter-animating-apps`, `flutter-building-layouts`

Load shared context: [M1.4 reading polish context](../instructions/m1.4-reading-polish-context.instructions.md)

---

## Task

Create a `ReadingMode` enum with a Riverpod provider, plus two new reading view widgets: sentence mode (shows the full sentence with current-word highlighting) and paragraph mode (shows the full paragraph with current-word highlighting and auto-scroll). These views are **standalone widgets** that accept props — they do NOT import each other or the reading screen. The `WordTimerNotifier` continues advancing word-by-word regardless of mode; these views just show more context around the current word.

**Key design insight:** The timer controls pacing; the view controls context. No timer logic changes needed.

## Files to Create

### 1. `lib/features/reading/providers/reading_mode_provider.dart`

```dart
import 'package:riverpod_annotation/riverpod_annotation.dart';

part 'reading_mode_provider.g.dart';

/// The available reading modes.
enum ReadingMode {
  /// Word-at-a-time RSVP with ORP — the default.
  rsvp,

  /// Full sentence with current word highlighted.
  sentence,

  /// Full paragraph with current word highlighted and auto-scroll.
  paragraph,
}

/// Provides the current reading mode. Auto-disposed when the reading
/// screen is unmounted (session-only, not persisted in AppConfig).
@riverpod
class ReadingModeNotifier extends _$ReadingModeNotifier {
  @override
  ReadingMode build() => ReadingMode.rsvp;

  /// Switch to a specific reading mode.
  void setMode(ReadingMode mode) {
    state = mode;
  }

  /// Cycle to the next mode: rsvp → sentence → paragraph → rsvp.
  void cycleMode() {
    state = ReadingMode.values[
      (state.index + 1) % ReadingMode.values.length
    ];
  }
}
```

**After creating this file, run `dart run build_runner build --delete-conflicting-outputs`.**

### 2. `lib/features/reading/widgets/sentence_mode_view.dart`

```dart
/// Displays the current sentence in full, centered in the viewport.
///
/// The current word is highlighted with bold + underline (not color-only).
/// The WordTimerNotifier continues advancing word-by-word; this view just
/// shows the full sentence for context.
class SentenceModeView extends StatelessWidget {
  const SentenceModeView({
    super.key,
    required this.words,
    required this.currentIndex,
    required this.letterSpacing,
    required this.wordSpacing,
    required this.fontFamily,
    required this.fontSize,
  });

  /// All words in the current document/slice.
  final List<String> words;

  /// The current word index (from WordTimerState.currentIndex).
  final int currentIndex;

  /// Extra letter spacing from accessibility settings.
  final double letterSpacing;

  /// Extra word spacing from accessibility settings.
  final double wordSpacing;

  /// Font family from config (e.g., 'BricolageGrotesque').
  final String fontFamily;

  /// Dynamic font size.
  final double fontSize;
}
```

**Sentence boundary detection — implement as private helper:**
```dart
/// Find the start and end indices (inclusive) of the sentence containing [wordIndex].
(int start, int end) _findSentenceBounds(List<String> words, int wordIndex) {
  // Scan backward for sentence-ending punctuation
  int start = wordIndex;
  while (start > 0 && !_isSentenceEnd(words[start - 1])) {
    start--;
  }
  // Scan forward for sentence-ending punctuation
  int end = wordIndex;
  while (end < words.length - 1 && !_isSentenceEnd(words[end])) {
    end++;
  }
  return (start, end);
}

bool _isSentenceEnd(String word) {
  return word.endsWith('.') || word.endsWith('!') || word.endsWith('?');
}
```

**Build method requirements:**
1. Call `_findSentenceBounds(words, currentIndex)` to get sentence range
2. Build a `Text.rich(TextSpan(...))` with:
   - Normal words: `RunThruTypography.body` base style, adjusted with `letterSpacing`, `wordSpacing`, `fontFamily`, `fontSize`
   - Current word: **bold + underline** (not color-only). Use `fontWeight: FontWeight.bold` and `decoration: TextDecoration.underline`
3. Center the text in the viewport: `Center(child: Padding(padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 24), child: text))`
4. Add `Semantics(label: 'Sentence mode. Current word: ${words[currentIndex]}')`
5. Handle edge case: if `currentIndex >= words.length`, show empty state

### 3. `lib/features/reading/widgets/paragraph_mode_view.dart`

```dart
/// Displays the current paragraph in full with current word highlighted.
///
/// Paragraphs are identified by scanning for empty-string entries or
/// words starting with newline characters. Falls back to ~50-word chunks
/// if no paragraph boundaries exist.
class ParagraphModeView extends StatelessWidget {
  const ParagraphModeView({
    super.key,
    required this.words,
    required this.currentIndex,
    required this.letterSpacing,
    required this.wordSpacing,
    required this.fontFamily,
    required this.fontSize,
  });

  // ... same parameters as SentenceModeView ...
}
```

**Paragraph boundary detection — implement as private helper:**
```dart
/// Find start and end of the paragraph containing [wordIndex].
///
/// Paragraphs are separated by empty-string entries or words that
/// start with '\n'. Falls back to 50-word chunks if no boundaries found.
(int start, int end) _findParagraphBounds(List<String> words, int wordIndex) {
  // Check if any paragraph boundaries exist
  bool hasBoundaries = words.any((w) => w.isEmpty || w.startsWith('\n'));

  if (!hasBoundaries) {
    // Fallback: 50-word chunks
    int chunkStart = (wordIndex ~/ 50) * 50;
    int chunkEnd = (chunkStart + 49).clamp(0, words.length - 1);
    return (chunkStart, chunkEnd);
  }

  // Scan backward for paragraph boundary
  int start = wordIndex;
  while (start > 0 && !_isParagraphBoundary(words[start - 1])) {
    start--;
  }

  // Scan forward for paragraph boundary
  int end = wordIndex;
  while (end < words.length - 1 && !_isParagraphBoundary(words[end + 1])) {
    end++;
  }

  return (start, end);
}

bool _isParagraphBoundary(String word) {
  return word.isEmpty || word == '\n' || word.startsWith('\n\n');
}
```

**Build method requirements:**
1. Call `_findParagraphBounds(words, currentIndex)` to get paragraph range
2. Build a `Text.rich(TextSpan(...))` — same highlighting as sentence mode (bold + underline on current word)
3. Wrap in `SingleChildScrollView` for long paragraphs
4. Line height: `height: 1.6` on the base TextStyle (comfortable reading)
5. Auto-scroll to keep current word visible — use a `ScrollController` with `WidgetsBinding.instance.addPostFrameCallback` to scroll after build. Since this is a `StatelessWidget`, use a `Builder` or convert to `StatefulWidget` only if scroll tracking requires it. Alternatively, use `Scrollable.ensureVisible` on the current word's `GlobalKey`.
6. `Semantics(label: 'Paragraph mode. Current word: ${words[currentIndex]}')`
7. Padding: `EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 24, vertical: 16)`

**Note on auto-scroll:** If implementing auto-scroll cleanly requires `StatefulWidget`, that is an **acceptable exception** to the "No StatefulWidget" project rule for this widget only — scroll position is ephemeral local state, not shared state. Do NOT use `setState()` for anything that other widgets observe.

---

## What NOT to Do

- Do NOT modify `WordTimerNotifier` or `WordTimerState`
- Do NOT modify `lib/core/orp.dart` or `lib/three_d/`
- Do NOT modify `lib/screens/reading_screen.dart` — R4 handles integration
- Do NOT persist `ReadingMode` in `AppConfig` — it's session-only (auto-dispose)
- Do NOT create separate timers for sentence/paragraph modes
- Do NOT import `reading_screen.dart` from any of these files
- Do NOT use raw `Color(0xFF...)` or raw `TextStyle()` — use design tokens
- Do NOT use deprecated `AnimatedBuilder` — use `ListenableBuilder` if needed
- Do NOT forget to run `build_runner` after creating the provider

---

## Verification

```powershell
dart run build_runner build --delete-conflicting-outputs
dart analyze lib/features/reading/
flutter test
```

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `ReadingMode` enum has `rsvp`, `sentence`, `paragraph`
- [ ] `ReadingModeNotifier` has `setMode()` and `cycleMode()` methods
- [ ] `.g.dart` file generated successfully by build_runner
- [ ] `SentenceModeView` displays full sentence centered with current word bold + underlined
- [ ] Sentence boundary detection works (scan for `.`, `!`, `?`)
- [ ] `ParagraphModeView` displays full paragraph with current word highlighted
- [ ] Paragraph boundary detection works with fallback to 50-word chunks
- [ ] Paragraph view has `SingleChildScrollView` for overflow
- [ ] Line height ≥ 1.5 on paragraph text
- [ ] Both views apply `letterSpacing` and `wordSpacing` from params
- [ ] Both views use `RunThruTypography.body` as base style
- [ ] Both views have `Semantics` labels
- [ ] Edge case: `currentIndex >= words.length` handled gracefully
- [ ] All colors from `RunThruTokens`, all text from `RunThruTypography`
- [ ] All public symbols have dartdoc comments
- [ ] `dart analyze` — zero issues
- [ ] `flutter test` — no new failures

- `lib/features/reading/providers/` directory doesn't exist — create it
- `lib/features/reading/widgets/` directory doesn't exist — create it
- A `reading_mode_provider.dart` already exists — verify and extend

List the ambiguities instead of guessing.
